overview: The Village of Kronenwetter Police and Fire Commission voted 4-0 to delay action on revisions to its Citizen Complaint/Charge Form, pending incorporation of additional changes discussed at the meeting. The commission also reviewed but took no action on a proposed new selection process for hiring fire department members. A community member raised concerns about electric scooters on Village streets during public comment.

agenda.item: New Business: Discussion and possible action after review of the Selection Process for Hiring Fire Department Members
Old Business: Discussion and possible action regarding revision to Commission's complaint form

discussions.body: Vice President Rick Smith moved, seconded by Commissioner Chet Rucinski, to approve the minutes of both prior meetings as submitted. The motion carried 3-0 by voice vote, with Commissioner Lucene Udulutch abstaining.

discussions.item: Selection Process for Hiring Fire Department Members
discussions.body: Chief O'Brien presented a proposal to have a fire department chief or fire officer meet with new applicants before their formal interview with the Police and Fire Commission. Commissioners discussed the concept but took no action.

discussions.item: Revision to Commission's Citizen Complaint/Charge Form
discussions.body: Commissioners reviewed attorney-proposed changes to the Citizen Complaint/Charge Form and discussed additional modifications they wished to incorporate. President Vedvik moved, seconded by Vice President Smith, to delay action pending incorporation of the changes discussed. The motion carried 4-0 by voice vote.

publicComment: William Udulutch of 1859 Kimberly Road, Kronenwetter, addressed the commission with two suggestions: that commissioners participate in ride-alongs with the Kronenwetter Police Department, and that the commission address electric scooters, which he described as a growing problem on Village streets.
actionItems: Staff to incorporate commissioner-discussed changes into the Citizen Complaint/Charge Form before the commission takes final action on the revised document.
No action taken on fire department hiring selection process; discussion to continue at a future meeting.

votes.motion: Approve the 04/27/2026 and 06/01/2026 meeting minutes as submitted
Delay action pending incorporating changes as discussed to Citizen Complaint/Charge Form

title: Police and Fire Commission Meeting - 2026-06-22
